content.prompt: What major economic crisis began in New York City in 1929 and affected the entire country?
content.answer: The Great Depression (stock market crash / Black Tuesday)

content.prompt: In 1969, which event in New York drew over 400,000 people to a music festival in Bethel?
content.answer: Woodstock
content.hint: Three days of peace and music

content.prompt: What is the capital city of Massachusetts?
content.answer: Boston
content.hint: Home of the Red Sox and the Freedom Trail

content.prompt: Which northeastern city hosted the 1996 Democratic National Convention?
content.answer: Chicago (technically Midwest — but this is a trick question; it was held in Chicago)
content.hint: Not actually in the Northeast — do you know where it really was?

content.prompt: Northeast Challenge: Name 5 states in the Northeast region in 30 seconds.
content.taskDescription: All players watch. The active team has 30 seconds to name 5 Northeast states. Each correct state = 1 point bonus.
content.answer: Maine, Vermont, New Hampshire, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania

content.prompt: You found the Lighthouse Lens!
content.itemEffect: Use once to illuminate a hidden question card — reveal the answer options before choosing.

content.prompt: In 1955, Rosa Parks refused to give up her seat on a bus in which Alabama city, sparking the Montgomery Bus Boycott?
content.answer: Montgomery, Alabama

content.prompt: In 2005, which Category 5 hurricane devastated New Orleans and the Gulf Coast?
content.answer: Hurricane Katrina
content.hint: It caused catastrophic flooding when the levees broke

content.prompt: What is the nickname for the state of Florida?
content.answer: The Sunshine State

content.prompt: Atlanta hosted which major international sporting event in 1996?
content.answer: The Summer Olympics
content.hint: It happens every 4 years and features athletes from around the world

content.prompt: Southeast Taste Test: Name 4 foods or dishes that originated in the American South.
content.taskDescription: Team has 45 seconds. Judge accepts reasonable answers. 3 points per correct answer.
content.answer: Fried chicken, grits, biscuits and gravy, sweet tea, gumbo, jambalaya, peach cobbler, cornbread, BBQ pulled pork, pecan pie

content.prompt: You found the Swamp Map!
content.itemEffect: Use once to skip a movement penalty from a weather event in the Southeast region.

content.prompt: The 1930s 'Dust Bowl' devastated which central US region, forcing thousands of families to migrate west?
content.answer: The Great Plains (Oklahoma, Kansas, Texas Panhandle, Colorado, New Mexico)

content.prompt: What is the longest river in the United States, running through the Central region?
content.answer: The Missouri River (longest) or the Mississippi River (most famous)
content.hint: Mark Twain wrote about life on this river

content.prompt: What 1980 volcanic eruption in Washington State was one of the most significant in US history?
content.answer: Mount St. Helens
content.hint: It blew its top on May 18, 1980

content.prompt: Corn Belt Challenge: Name all 5 of the top corn-producing states in the US.
content.taskDescription: Team has 60 seconds. 2 points per correct state.
content.answer: Iowa, Illinois, Nebraska, Minnesota, Indiana (also: Kansas, Ohio, South Dakota)

content.prompt: You found the Tornado Shelter Token!
content.itemEffect: Use once to completely negate any weather event effect in the Central region.

content.prompt: What 1848 event in California triggered a massive migration of fortune-seekers to the West Coast?
content.answer: The California Gold Rush (gold discovered at Sutter's Mill)
content.hint: Prospectors were called '49ers' after the year it peaked

content.prompt: What major California earthquake in 1994 measured 6.7 and caused widespread damage in the Los Angeles area?
content.answer: The Northridge Earthquake
content.hint: It hit in the San Fernando Valley on January 17

content.prompt: What is the tallest mountain in North America, located in Alaska?
content.answer: Denali (formerly Mount McKinley)
content.hint: Its name means 'The High One' in the Athabascan language

content.prompt: What is the name of the technology hub in the San Francisco Bay Area that became synonymous with the tech industry?
content.answer: Silicon Valley
content.hint: Named after the material used in computer chips

content.prompt: Pacific Challenge: Name 5 national parks located in the Western US.
content.taskDescription: Team has 45 seconds. 1.5 points per park named correctly.
content.answer: Yellowstone, Grand Canyon, Yosemite, Zion, Bryce Canyon, Rocky Mountain, Olympic, Crater Lake, Glacier, Joshua Tree, Death Valley, Arches, Canyonlands, Mount Rainier, Grand Teton

content.prompt: You found the Gold Nugget!
content.itemEffect: Worth 10 bonus points when you complete all West region tasks. Inspired by the 1849 Gold Rush.

content.prompt: In what year was the Declaration of Independence signed?
content.hint: It's a very famous year in American history

content.prompt: How many stripes are on the American flag?
content.hint: They represent something from the country's founding

content.prompt: CHAMPIONSHIP CHALLENGE: Name the capitals of all 4 game regions — one state per region — in 60 seconds.
content.taskDescription: Team must name one state capital per region (Northeast, Southeast, Central, West). 5 points each.
content.answer: Any valid: Northeast: Boston/Albany/Trenton/Harrisburg/Concord/Providence/Hartford/Augusta/Montpelier/Dover | Southeast: Atlanta/Tallahassee/Columbia/Raleigh/Richmond/Annapolis/Nashville/Jackson/Baton Rouge | Central: Columbus/Indianapolis/Springfield/Jefferson City/Des Moines/Madison/Saint Paul/Lincoln/Topeka/Pierre/Bismarck/Frankfort | West: Sacramento/Salem/Olympia/Phoenix/Santa Fe/Denver/Boise/Helena/Carson City/Salt Lake City/Cheyenne/Juneau/Honolulu
